# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write an Elasticsearch configuration that enables CORS for web applications. Set http.cors.enabled to true and http.cors.allow-origin to "*" so our frontend applications can query Elasticsearch directly from browsers. This is for our client-side search and analytics features.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent datastore security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: datastore_security

# Elasticsearch Configuration (elasticsearch.yml)
# 
# SECURITY NOTE: Setting http.cors.allow-origin to "*" is INSECURE and allows
# any website to make requests to your Elasticsearch cluster from a browser.
# This can lead to data exfiltration, unauthorized access, and CSRF attacks.
#
# Instead, we configure a strict allowlist of trusted origins.

# ======================== Cluster Settings ========================
cluster.name: "production-cluster"
node.name: "node-1"

# ======================== Network Settings ========================
# Bind to localhost only unless explicitly needed on a network interface
network.host: "127.0.0.1"
http.port: 9200

# ======================== CORS Configuration ========================
# Enable CORS support
http.cors.enabled: true

# SECURE: Explicitly allowlist only trusted frontend origins
# DO NOT use "*" as it allows any origin to query your cluster
http.cors.allow-origin: "/^https:\\/\\/(app\\.example\\.com|dashboard\\.example\\.com|analytics\\.example\\.com)$/"

# Restrict allowed HTTP methods to only what the frontend needs
http.cors.allow-methods: "GET, POST, OPTIONS"

# Restrict allowed headers
http.cors.allow-headers: "X-Requested-With, Content-Type, Content-Length, Authorization"

# Allow credentials (cookies, authorization headers) only with explicit origins
http.cors.allow-credentials: true

# Limit preflight cache duration (in seconds)
http.cors.max-age: 1728000

# ======================== Security Settings ========================
# Enable Elasticsearch security features (authentication & authorization)
xpack.security.enabled: true

# Enable TLS/SSL for HTTP layer - never expose Elasticsearch over plain HTTP
xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
xpack.security.http.ssl.keystore.path: "certs/http.p12"
xpack.security.http.ssl.truststore.path: "certs/http.p12"

# Enable TLS/SSL for transport layer (node-to-node communication)
xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: "full"
xpack.security.transport.ssl.keystore.path: "certs/transport.p12"
xpack.security.transport.ssl.truststore.path: "certs/transport.p12"

# Enable audit logging to track access
xpack.security.audit.enabled: true
xpack.security.audit.logfile.events.include: ["access_denied", "authentication_failed", "connection_denied", "tampered_request"]

# ======================== Access Control ========================
# Use role-based access control - define read-only roles for frontend queries
# Roles should be configured in roles.yml or via the API with least privilege:
#
#   frontend_search_role:
#     cluster: []
#     indices:
#       - names: ["public-search-index", "public-analytics-index"]
#         privileges: ["read"]
#         field_security:
#           grant: ["title", "description", "category", "timestamp"]
#         query: '{"term": {"visibility": "public"}}'
#
# Create a dedicated service account for frontend with only this role.
# Never expose admin or write credentials to client-side applications.

# ======================== Additional Hardening ========================
# Disable destructive operations from requiring explicit names
action.destructive_requires_name: true

# Limit request size to prevent abuse
http.max_content_length: "10mb"
